name: Session Manager
description: Manages the lifecycle of Claude and shell terminal sessions. Persists session state per-repo in VS Code globalState, launches terminals with generated MCP config, tracks live terminal activity for the blink indicator, and handles per-worktree preferences (model, effort, yolo, color).
guidelines: All session state is stored under a single REPOS_KEY in globalState keyed by git common-dir so all worktrees of the same repo share one bucket.
Normalize worktree paths via normalizeWtPath before any equality check (handles trailing slash, case folding, platform separators).
Run migrateLegacyIntoRepo once per repo to absorb old v1/v2 workspaceState data into the repo bucket.
anti_patterns: Do not use workspaceState for new session/prefs storage — it fragments across folder-identity windows. Always use globalState REPOS_KEY.
Do not assume Terminal.name stays in sync automatically; sync it on focus/state change events via syncTerminalNames().
decisions: Activity indicator uses a blink timer at 500 ms intervals driven by onDidWriteTerminalData (proposed API); the feature degrades gracefully if the API is unavailable.
setActiveWorktree opens a new VS Code window rather than mutating the current workspace, so each window owns exactly one worktree.
